launchStatus.title: First Saturday Snow Ranch Launch
launchStatus.message: Snow Ranch is the next launch site. Gates open at 9:00 AM and flying runs until 4:00 PM.

weatherForecast.summary: Mostly sunny with light afternoon breezes.

launchStatus.fallbackText: Next launch date is being finalized. Check back soon.

alert.headline: Launch cancelled due to weather
alert.body: The range is closed due to sustained high winds. We will post a make-up date by noon.

flightHistory.title: March Wind Window Launch
flightHistory.summary: 18 flyers, 61 total flights, one Level 1 cert success and 100 percent recovery rate.

flightHistory.title: Winter Precision Day
flightHistory.summary: Chip Can challenge test flights, low ceiling adaptation drills, and avionics workshop in the prep tent.

flightHistory.title: New Year Range Kickoff
flightHistory.summary: First launch of the year with 47 flights. Outreach table hosted 23 first-time guests.

launches.title: First Saturday Launch
launches.description: Primary club launch at Snow Ranch with all flight classes and cert attempts allowed.

launches.title: Brigantino Community Launch
launches.description: Smaller-field launch with emphasis on lower impulse flights and family viewing.

launches.title: ARC Team Flight Day
launches.description: American Rocketry Challenge only session for student teams and mentors.

faq.question: Do I need prior rocketry experience to fly with LUNAR?
faq.answer: No. New flyers are welcome, and mentors can help with your first prep, safety check, and launch card.

faq.question: How are cancellations or launch moves communicated?
faq.answer: The homepage alert banner updates first, then the same status is posted in Launch Updates and the club mailing list.

faq.question: Can guests attend launch days?
faq.answer: Yes. Spectators are always welcome. Guest flyers can fly with a day fee and must complete the range waiver.

faq.question: How do High Power certifications work?
faq.answer: L1 and L2 cert attempts are flown at sanctioned launches with certified witnesses. L3 requires a review panel and advance planning.

faq.question: Where can I find launch summaries and historical records?
faq.answer: Use the Records and History page for flight summaries, counters, and the current record leaderboard.

lostFound.notes: Blue heat-shrink and silver tape on antenna base.
Includes Raven board and two LiPo straps.

programs.name: American Rocketry Challenge
programs.description: Mentored student teams design, build, and fly payload missions for national competition.
highlights: Weekly design review nights during season
Dedicated practice windows at monthly launches
Mentor pool for simulation, recovery, and test planning

programs.name: High Power Certification
programs.description: A structured pathway for L1-L3 prep, safety review, and certification attempts.
highlights: Pre-flight readiness checklist and mentor signoff
Post-flight debriefs to improve every attempt

programs.name: Chip Can Challenge
programs.description: A precision-build challenge focused on lightweight design, repeatability, and landing control.
highlights: Shared scoring rubric for altitude and recovery quality
Open build sessions with rapid iteration support
Season standings tracked on Records and History

officers.name: Marcus Rivera
officers.focus: Programming, workshops, and launch day logistics
